zh.lifeSummary: 當Adelize FitzRichard de Clare於大約1068年出生在英國英格蘭肯特坦布利其時，她的父親Richard FitzGilbert de Clare39歲，母親Rohese Giffard35歲。她和Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix至少有6個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於從1125年到1135年，埋葬在法國瑟-吉瓦康福藍-賽特-諾利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adelize FitzRichard de Clare于大约1068年出生在英国英格兰肯特坦布利其时，她的父亲Richard FitzGilbert de Clare39岁，母亲Rohese Giffard35岁。她和Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix至少有6个儿子和5个女儿。她去世于从1125年到1135年，埋葬在法国瑟-吉瓦康福藍-賽特-諾利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Adelize
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: diminutive of Adèle ( see Adele ), used occasionally in England from the early 16th century onwards, and now also found in the wider English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Adelize FitzRichard de Clare was born about 1068, in Tunbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard FitzGilbert de Clare, was 39 and her mother, Rohese Giffard, was 35. She had at least 6 sons and 5 daughters with Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix. She died from 1125 to 1135, and was buried in Conflans-Sainte-Honorine, Seine-et-Oise, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adelize FitzRichard de Clare est née environ 1068, à Tunbridge, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard FitzGilbert de Clare, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Rohese Giffard, avait 35 ans. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles avec Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix. Elle est décédée du 1125 au 1135, et a été enterrée à Conflans-Sainte-Honorine, Seine-et-Oise, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adelize FitzRichard de Clare um 1068 in Tunbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard FitzGilbert de Clare 39 und ihre Mutter Rohese Giffard 35. Sie hatte mit Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix mindestens 6 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb von 1125 bis 1135 und wurde in Conflans-Sainte-Honorine, Seine-et-Oise, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adelize FitzRichard de Clare è nata il circa 1068 nel Tunbridge, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard FitzGilbert de Clare, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Rohese Giffard, aveva 35 anni. Lei avuto almeno 6 figli e 5 figlie con Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix. Morì da 1125 a 1135, e fu sepolta a Conflans-Sainte-Honorine, Seine-et-Oise, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Adelize FitzRichard de Clareが約1068年にイギリスイギリスケントトゥンブリッジで生まれた時，父Richard FitzGilbert de Clareは39歳，母Rohese Giffardは35歳であった。彼女はSir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poixとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼女はから1125年まで1135年に死去し，フランスセアイネ-エト-オイゼコンフランス-サインテ-hオノーアインにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Adelize FitzRichard de Clare(이)가 대략 1068년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 툰브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard FitzGilbert de Clare(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rohese Giffard(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1125년 부터 1135년 까지 사망했으며 프랑스 세이인-트-이스 콘플랜스-세인테-호노라이인에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adelize FitzRichard de Clare nasceu aproximadamente 1068, em Tunbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard FitzGilbert de Clare, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Rohese Giffard, tinha 35 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas com Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix. Ela faleceu de 1125 a 1135, e foi sepultada em Conflans-Sainte-Honorine, Seine-et-Oise, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adelize FitzRichard de Clare родилась около 1068 г. в местности тунбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard FitzGilbert de Clare было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Rohese Giffard, 35. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей от Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix. Она умерла с 1125 по 1135 и была похоронена в местности конфланс-сеинте-хонораин, сине-ет-оис, Франция.

sm.Abt 1068–from 1125 to 1135: e tusa o le 1068–mai le 1125 e o'o I le 1135

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adelize FitzRichard de Clare nació aproximadamente 1068, en Tunbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard FitzGilbert de Clare, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Rohese Giffard, tenía 35 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas con Sir Walter Tyrell III Vicomte d’Amiens 3e seigneur de Poix. Murió desde 1125 hasta 1135, y fue sepultada en Conflans-Sainte-Honorine, Seine-et-Oise, Francia.
